title: CCPA Consumer Data Access and Deletion Request Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: CCPA/CPRA §1798.100 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA/CPRA §1798.100 (Consumer right to know) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.4.1: CCPA Consumer Data Access and Deletion Request Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.4.1' running on sourcetype snow:sc_req_item and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CCPA/CPRA §1798.105 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA/CPRA §1798.105 (Consumer right to delete) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.4.1: CCPA Consumer Data Access and Deletion Request Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.4.1' running on sourcetype snow:sc_req_item and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, cat_item, short_description) or `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, short_description)
spl:
index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" OR sourcetype="snow:incident")
    (cat_item="*CCPA*" OR cat_item="*Privacy*" OR short_description="*CCPA*" OR short_description="*Consumer Privacy*")
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed_epoch=if(isnotnull(closed_at), strptime(closed_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null())
| eval age_days=round((now()-opened_epoch)/86400, 1)
| eval sla_days=45
| eval breach=if(isnull(closed_epoch) AND age_days>sla_days, 1, 0)
| eval days_remaining=if(isnull(closed_epoch), sla_days-age_days, null())
| table _time, number, state, age_days, days_remaining, breach, short_description
| sort - breach, days_remaining
description: Tracks privacy request fulfillment work items end-to-end and flags requests at risk of missing the 45-day statutory response window.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident, snow:sc_req_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Configure ServiceNow inputs for sc_req_item and/or incidents; (2) normalize catalog item names to match the filter (adjust `cat_item` strings); (3) if CCPA allows extensions, add fields for `extension_days` and update `sla_days` logic; (4) schedule daily with alert on `breach=1`. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`, `sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item`.
visualization: Table (open requests with SLA countdown), Histogram (age distribution), Single value (% within 45 days). Key fields: `age_days`, `breach`, `closed_epoch`, `days_remaining`, `number`, `opened_epoch`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, cat_item, short_description) or `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, short_description).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Configure ServiceNow inputs for sc_req_item and/or incidents; (2) normalize catalog item names to match the filter (adjust `cat_item` strings); (3) if CCPA allows extensions, add fields for `extension_days` and update `sla_days` logic; (4) schedule daily with alert on `breach=1`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" OR sourcetype="snow:incident")
    (cat_item="*CCPA*" OR cat_item="*Privacy*" OR short_description="*CCPA*" OR short_description="*Consumer Privacy*")
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed_epoch=if(isnotnull(closed_at), strptime(closed_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null())
| eval age_days=round((now()-opened_epoch)/86400, 1)
| eval sla_days=45
| eval breach=if(isnull(closed_epoch) AND age_days>sla_days, 1, 0)
| eval days_remaining=if(isnull(closed_epoch), sla_days-age_days, null())
| table _time, number, state, age_days, days_remaining, breach, short_description
| sort - breach, days_remaining
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA Consumer Data Access and Deletion Request Tracking (§1798.100-105)** — Tracks privacy request fulfillment work items end-to-end and flags requests at risk of missing the 45-day statutory response window.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, cat_item, short_description) or `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, short_description). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:sc_req_item, snow:incident. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opened_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **closed_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_remaining** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**CCPA Consumer Data Access and Deletion Request Tracking (§1798.100-105)** — Tracks privacy request fulfillment work items end-to-end and flags requests at risk of missing the 45-day statutory response window.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, cat_item, short_description) or `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, short_description). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, age_days, breach, closed_epoch, days_remaining, number, opened_epoch, short_description.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open requests with SLA countdown), Histogram (age distribution), Single value (% within 45 days).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for tracks privacy request fulfillment work items end-to-end and flags requests at risk of missing the 45-day statutory response window. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
